title: Gecco 2026: Evolutionary Computation in Practice
text:
Bridging Academia and Industry in Evolutionary Computation
The Evolutionary Computation in Practice (ECiP) track at GECCO brings together world-renowned experts from academia and industry to share their insights on real-world optimization and successful collaborations. ECiP is the place to learn how evolutionary computation is applied beyond textbooks, with a focus on practical, impactful projects and industrial partnerships.

title: Gecco 2026: Evolutionary Computation in Practice
text:
1.1 About GECCO
GECCO is sponsored by the Association for Computing Machinery Special Interest Group on Genetic and Evolutionary Computation (SIGEVO).

title: ECiP Speakers & Talks
text:
The Evolutionary Computation in Practice (ECiP) track at GECCO bridges the gap between theoretical research and industrial application. Below is a historical archive of the track’s chairs, organizers, and invited speakers.




Thomas Bartz-Beielstein IDE+A, TH Köln, Germany


Richard Schulz IDE+A, TH Köln, Germany





Organizers: Thomas Bartz-Beielstein, Richard Schulz, Danial Yazdani.


Thomas Bartz-Beielstein IDE+A, TH Köln, Germany


Richard Schulz IDE+A, TH Köln, Germany


Danial Yazdani University of Technology Sydney, Australia





Organizers for these years included Thomas Bartz-Beielstein, Bogdan Filipic, Richard Schulz, and Sowmya Chandrasekaran.


Thomas Bartz-Beielstein IDE+A, TH Köln, Germany


Bogdan Filipic Jozef Stefan Institute, Slovenia


Sowmya Chandrasekaran TH Köln, Germany

“Surrogate Model-based Optimization in Practice”

Bogdan Filipic Jozef Stefan Institute, Slovenia

“Challenges of Deploying EC in Steel Industry”



Carlos A. Coello Coello CINVESTAV-IPN, Mexico

“EMO in Real-World Applications”








Michael Affenzeller UAS Upper Austria

“Heuristic Optimization in Production and Logistics”



Thomas Bäck Leiden University / divis

“Intelligent Industry Solutions”



Erik Goodman Michigan State University

“Introducing Academic EC Tech to Industry”
